title: 10 medicinal constituents of Digitalis lanata (foxglove)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Digitalis lanata digoxin is a cardiac glycoside extracted from the leaves of the plant. It has been used for centuries to treat various heart conditions, including atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. Digoxin works by slowing down the heart rate and increasing the contraction force, allowing the heart to pump more efficiently. As an oral medication, it is often prescribed in combination with other treatments to manage cardiac arrhythmias and improve overall heart function.

Digitalis lanata digitoxin is a cardiac glycoside extracted from the leaves of the plant. It has been used for centuries to treat various heart conditions, such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. Digitoxin works by increasing the force of contractions in the heart and slowing down the heart rate, thereby improving cardiac function. Its exact mechanism of action is still not fully understood, but it is considered a valuable medication for managing certain cardiovascular disorders.

Digitalis lanata gitoxigenin is a cardiac glycoside extracted from the leaves of the plant. It is a powerful natural compound that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various heart conditions, including atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. Gitoxigenin works by increasing the force of contraction of the heart muscle, allowing it to pump more efficiently and effectively.

Digitalis lanata deslanoside is a cardiac glycoside extracted from the leaves of the plant. It is used in medicine to treat certain heart conditions, such as atrial fibrillation and atrial flutter, by slowing down the heart rate and increasing its efficiency. Deslanoside has been shown to be effective in reducing symptoms and improving quality of life for patients with heart failure.

Digitalis lanata lanatosides is a glycoside compound extracted from the leaves of the plant. It is a complex mixture of cardenolides, including digoxin and digitoxin, which are used to treat cardiac arrhythmias and congestive heart failure. Lanatosides have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and may also play a role in the treatment of certain cancers. The exact mechanisms of action for lanatosides are not fully understood, but they are known to interact with various ion channels and receptors in the body.

Digitalis lanata digoxigenin-3-glucuronide is a naturally occurring compound found in the leaves of the plant. It is a glycoside that plays a crucial role in the defense mechanism of the plant against pathogens and insects. The compound has been isolated and purified for its potential therapeutic applications, particularly in the treatment of cardiac arrhythmias and congestive heart failure. Its unique structure and properties make it an attractive candidate for further research and development as a medicinal agent.

Digitalis lanata gitoxigenin-3-glucuronide is a naturally occurring diglycoside that is derived from the leaves of the plant. It is a key component of Digitalis purpurea, a cardiac glycoside used in medicine to treat atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. Gitoxigenin-3-glucuronide has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable compound for further research and development.

Digitalis lanata cymarin is a naturally occurring cardiac glycoside extracted from the leaves of Digitalis purpurea, a plant native to Europe. Cymarin has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various heart conditions, such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. Its mechanism of action involves binding to sodium-potassium pumps, slowing down the heart rate, and increasing cardiac contractility, making it an effective treatment for heart rhythm disorders.

Digitalis lanata digoxenin is a cardiac glycoside extracted from the leaves of the plant. It is used to treat various heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure by regulating the heart's rhythm and increasing its contraction force. Digoxenin is often administered orally or intravenously in the form of pills, elixirs, or injections, and its effects are closely monitored due to its narrow therapeutic index and potential toxicity at high doses.

Digitalis lanata gitoxin is a toxic glycoside found in the leaves of the plant. It is responsible for the cardiotonic effects of the plant, which have been used for centuries to treat various heart conditions, including atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. Gitoxin works by slowing down the heartbeat, allowing the heart to pump more efficiently and increasing cardiac output. However, it can also cause serious side effects if not used properly, making it a complex medication with a narrow therapeutic index.

intro: Digitalis lanata has active constituents such as cardiac glycosides, specifically digoxin and digitoxin. These compounds possess potent medicinal properties, exhibiting cardiotonic and antiarrhythmic effects, which aid in the treatment of heart failure, atrial fibrillation, and other cardiac disorders. By improving heart function and regulating heartbeat, these constituents can significantly improve quality of life for individuals with cardiovascular conditions, enabling them to lead more active and healthy lifestyles.
intro_desc: Digitalis lanata has active constituents such as digoxin, digoxigenin, and digitoxin, which possess medicinal properties that treat heart conditions, particularly atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. These constituents help regulate heartbeat, increase cardiac contractility, and reduce cardiac workload. As a result, patients with heart failure can experience improved symptoms and enhanced quality of life. Furthermore, these constituents can also be used to treat certain arrhythmias and reduce the risk of sudden cardiac death.
